projects.name: ⭐️ Insurance Slip Data Extraction
projects.description: “Insurance Slip Data Extraction” ingests insurance documents, then classifies (slip/endorsement/other; insurance/reinsurance; treaty/fac; proportionality) and extracts structured underwriting data (UMR, underwriter, broker, insured/reinsured, classes, terms, limits currency, dates/times JSON, placement, line basis, written line, commissions/deductions, order). Uses D&B-backed web search to derive broker country; outputs standardized, structured fields.

properties.name: Classification
properties.description:
You are an expert in the insurance industry. Your task is to classify the provided document as one of slip, endorsement or other. 
* A slip will layout the terms by which an insurance policy will be written. 
* An endorsement will set out the change or adjustment to an existing insurance policy.
If the document does not fit into either of these 2 categories then you must classify it as other.

properties.name: Underwriter
properties.description: You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, you must extract the underwriter. This is the individual or entity responsible for evaluating and assuming the risk of insuring clients. Identified on the slip in the Security Details section. You must respond with the name of the underwriter only. If no underwriter is provided in the document, then you must respond with “N/A”.

properties.name: Broker Name
properties.description:
You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, you must extract the broker name. This is the intermediary representing the insured in securing insurance coverage. This is typically found in the header or logo on the slip. 
You must respond with the name of the broker only.

properties.name: Unique Market Reference
properties.description:
You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document,
What is the contract reference noted under the “Unique Market Reference Header”? If not provided in the document, then respond with “N/A”

properties.name: Original Insured
properties.description: You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, your task is to extract the name of the original insured company. This may also be know as the participant. You should not refer to any company named as the cedant or reinsured. Respond with the name of the original insured only.

properties.name: Reinsured Name
properties.description: You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, your task is to extract the name of the reinsured. This is the entity transferring risk to the reinsurer; recorded similarly to the Original Insured in the Risk Details section. You should not refer to any company named as the cedant or reinsured. Respond with the name of the reinsured only.

properties.name: Class Of Businesses
Class Of Business
properties.description: result = [@<Pproperty_qo7pTdTi6tlnKz7z>]

properties.name: Type Of Cover
Type Of Cover
properties.description: result = [@<Pproperty_OmFiZOhL_grOWAMR>]

properties.name: Limits, Excess, Deductible
Excess Type
properties.description: result = [@<Pproperty_ZGZmuhdvCulT4VJi>]

properties.name: Deductible Type
properties.description: result = [@<Pproperty_ZGZmuhdvCulT4VJi>]

You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided slip document, your task is to extract information relating specifically to the type of cover and subclass combination provided. Here is the type of cover:
<type_of_cover>
@<Pproperty_OmFiZOhL_grOWAMR>
</type_of_cover>
Here is the subclass:
<subclass>
@<Pproperty_LpQZFvhMPLeUTzFA>
<subclass>
For this type of cover and subclass provided, you must extract:
- Limit Amount
- Limit Original Currency
- Limit Type
- Excess Original Currency
- Deductible Original Currency
<guidelines>
1. Limit Type must only be one of “Any One Event - AOE”, “Any One Location - AOL”, “Each & Every - EAE”, “Ultimate Net Loss - UNL” or “Combined Single Limit - CSL”.
2. Limit original currency, excess original currency and deductible original currency must all be provided in ISO 4217 currency codes. If the original currency is not specified in the document, then respond with “N/A”.
3. Limit amount must be provided as a number only
</guidelines>
You must respond in JSON format, following this structure:
[
{
“Limit Amount”: [insert the limit amount],
“Limit Original Currency”: [insert the limit currency for the limit amount],
“Limit Type”: [insert the full limit type],
“Excess Original Currency”: [insert the excess original currency],
“Deductible Original Currency”: [insert the deductible original currency]
},
{
“Limit Amount”: [insert the limit amount],
“Limit Original Currency”: [insert the limit currency for the limit amount],
“Limit Type”: [insert the limit type],
“Excess Original Currency”: [insert the excess original currency],
“Deductible Original Currency”: [insert the deductible original currency]
},
…[continue extracting for each limit listed in the document]…
]
If any value is not provided in the document, then you must respond with “N/A” for that value. Remember, only extract the values for the provided for the provided type of cover and subclass.

properties.name: Premiums
Type
properties.description: result = [@<Pproperty_5XaRu5B9D8PpUz8R>]

properties.name: Manual ROE
properties.description: result = [@<Pproperty_WGxrLehGYi9dCXHm>]

You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided slip document, your task is to extract information relating specifically to the type of cover and subclass combination provided. Here is the type of cover:
<type_of_cover>
@<Pproperty_OmFiZOhL_grOWAMR>
</type_of_cover>
Here is the subclass:
<subclass>
@<Pproperty_LpQZFvhMPLeUTzFA>
<subclass>
For this combination, you must extract the following premium values:
- Premium Type
- Premium Currency
- Premium Rate / Amount
- Premium Amount
- Premium Manual ROE
- Basis Amount
<guidelines>
1. Premium Type is the whether the premium is a flat rate or based on minimum and deposit amounts. You must provide your answer as one “Flat Rate” or “Minimum & Deposit”
2. Premium currency must be provided in ISO 4217 currency codes. If the currency for the premium is not specified in the document, then respond with “N/A”.
3. Premium Rate / Amount must be only one of “Rate Percentage” or “Premium Amount”
4. Premium amount must be provided as either a % value (e.g. ‘3.4%’) or as a nominal number with no currency symbol)
5. Premium Manual ROE is the Manual Rate of Exchange for premium calculations. If this value is provided then respond with “Yes”, if not specified then respond with “No”.
6. If the premium rate/amount is “Rate Percentage”, then you must provide the basis amount for this premium in order to calculate the premium value. If the premium rate/amount is “Premium Amount” then respond with “N/A”. 
</guidelines>
You must respond in JSON format, following this structure:
[
{
“Premium Type”: [insert the premium type],
“Premium Currency”: [insert the premium currency],
“Premium Rate/Amount”: [insert one of “Rate” or “Amount”],
“Premium Amount”: [insert the premium amount],
“Premium Manual ROE”: [insert the premium manual rate of exchange],
“Basis Amount”: [insert the basis amount for the premium]
},
{
“Premium Type”: [insert the premium type],
“Premium Currency”: [insert the premium currency],
“Premium Rate/Amount”: [insert one of “Rate” or “Amount”],
“Premium Amount”: [insert the premium amount],
“Premium Manual ROE”: [insert the premium manual rate of exchange],
“Basis Amount”: [insert the basis amount for the premium]
},
…[continue extracting for each limit listed in the document]…
]
If any value is not provided in the document, then you must respond with “N/A” for that value. Remember, only extract the values for the specific type of cover and subclass provided. There must be at least 1 premium listed in the document.

properties.name: SubClass Options
SubClass
properties.description: result = [@<Pproperty_LpQZFvhMPLeUTzFA>]

properties.name: SubClass
properties.description:
You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, your task is to extract the sub class for this provided type of cover. The type of cover is @<Pproperty_0IQsdMFPoKwbVGUP>. Based on this, the subclass must be one of the following options:
<subclass_options>
@<Pproperty_aj64nvP5U3nDlcH3>
</subclass_options>
You must extract the subclass for the given type of cover in the provided slip document.

You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, your task is to extract each type of cover. The document might contain cover for one or many of the following types:
<types_of_cover>
@<Pproperty_ctIhn-o0mZ_jBZo_>
</types_of_cover>
You must extract each type of cover listed in the slip document. 
Remember it might be the case that only one type of cover is provided, or it might be the case that multiple types of cover are listed in the provided slip document. Each type of cover will always have an associated premium.

properties.name: ToC Options

properties.description:
What line or class of business does this insurance contract relate to. It might be the case that multiple classes of business are provided in this contract, in which case you must provide them all. The class of business can only be or multiple of:
* Surety
* Liability
* Marine Cargo
* Marine Hull
* Marine Liability
* Property
* Cyber
* Engineering
* Specie
* Aviation
* Personal Accident
* Motor
* Energy
* Terrorism
If any provided class of business does not match to one of the above options then provide the answer as “Other”. Remember it might be the case that only one class of business is provided, or it might be the case that multiple class of businesses are listed in the slip.

properties.name: Contract Type
properties.description: You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, your task is to classify the insurance document as relating to “Insurance” or “Reinsurance”. This can be determined by examining the Type on the slip document provided.

properties.name: Treaty/Facultative
properties.description:
You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, you must classify whether this slip is a Treaty or Facultative. This refers to the type of reinsurance. Treaty covers multiple risks, while Facultative covers individual risks. Determined from the Type section in the provided slip document.
If the @<Pproperty_WWuYyTmEFEUhXOeD> is not reinsurance then respond with “N/A”.

properties.name: Proportional or Non-Proportional
properties.description: You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, you must classify whether this slip has a proportional or non-proportional retention for the Cedent. This describes how losses are shared between the insurer and reinsurer. Proportional means shared based on agreed terms, while non-proportional refers to excess cover.

properties.name: Reinsurance Type
properties.description: You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, you must classify the type of reinsurance as one of “Quota Share”, “Excess of Loss”, “Surplus Treaty“ or “Stop Loss“.

properties.name: Inception Date & Expiry Date
properties.description:
You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, your task is to extract both the inception date, inception time and the expiry date and expiry time. This is the start and end dates of the coverage; found in the Risk Details section. You must provide the date in DD/MM/YYYY format and the time in hh:mm format. Your response must be JSON format, following this structure:
{
“Inception Date”: [insert the start date of the coverage],
“Inception Time”: [insert the start time of the coverage],
“Expiry Date”: [insert the end date of the coverage],
“Expiry Time”: [insert the end time of the coverage]
}
For any start time not stated in the document, you must respond with 00:01. 
For any end time not stated in the document, you must respond with 23:59.

properties.name: Contractual CCY
properties.description: You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, your task is to extract the contractual currency. This is the currency in which the contract is denominated; identified in the Limits section of the slip. Your response must be in ISO currency format, e.g. USD or GBP.

properties.name: Placement Type
properties.description: You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, your task is to extract the placement type only. This identifies whether insurance is placed as a 100% placement or subscription agreement based on the stamp details. If no placement type is provided, then respond with “N/A”

properties.name: Line Basis
properties.description: You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, your task is to extract the line basis only, as one of either “Whole” or “Order”. If line basis is not specified in the document, then you must respond with “Order”.

properties.name: Written Line
properties.description: You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, your task is to extract the written line only. This is the percentage of the total risk the insurer is responsible for under a subscription agreement. Your response must be provided as a %, e.g. “4%”.

properties.name: Broker Commission
properties.description: You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, your task is to extract the broker commission. This is the percentage of the premium paid to the broker; found in the deductions section. If no broker commission is provided, or if the broker commission is provided as a nominal sum, then you must respond with “N/A”. Remember, only provide the broker commission if the commission is provided as a percentage, and your response must only be that %, e.g. “4%”. Otherwise you must respond with “N/A”.

properties.name: Additional Deductions
properties.description:
You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, your task is to extract any additional deductions outside of broker commissions. These are the other fees deducted from the premium, not including broker commissions; specified in the deductions section. For each deduction you must provide:
- deduction name
- deduction percentage (this must be provided as a decimal with no commas)
- gross or net (whether the payment is calculated on a gross or net basis (this must be one of “Gross” or “Net”)

If broker commissions are provided as a % then they should not be included, however if they are provided as a nominal amount then you must include them as a deductible in your response.

properties.name: Broker Country
properties.description:
What is the country for the provided insurance broker. Here is the insurance broker legal name and provided MRC document.
<insurance broker>
@<Pproperty_RwsOkcs9lWxEBYgx>
</insurance broker>
Respond with the country only, use Duns & Bradstreet - Business Directory as your source.

properties.name: Order Percentage
properties.description: You are an expert in the insurance industry, specifically in underwriting. Based on the provided document, your task is to extract the order percentage only. This is the percentage of the total risk the broker is responsible for placing. Your response must be provided as a %, e.g. “4%”. If no explicit order percentage is provided then you must respond with “100%”.
